column1: # Wug test: pseudo-words for morphological generalization
#   expected: primary answer (pipe-separated if multiple are acceptable)
#   notes: which phonological cell is tested
#   - Berko (1958): the original wug study, items marked [Berko]
#   - Extensions: hand-curated to cover phonotactic cells Berko did not
# Note on "heaf -> heafs|heaves": Berko's empirical finding was that
# English-speaking children produce "heafs", showing they do NOT extend
# the f->v alternation (leaf->leaves) to novel words. A neural model may
# or may not generalize the rule. Both answers are accepted for scoring.

column4: [Berko] tests f->v rule; Berko found children say "heafs"
